claim.content: For the fully spin-polarized (ferromagnetic, ζ = 1) two-dimensional uniform electron liquid (2D-UEL) studied in the metallic density range 1 ≤ r_s ≤ 5, VMC and fixed-node DMC calculations performed for simulation cells containing N = 151 electrons indicate that the quasiparticle effective mass m* decreases as r_s increases (i.e., m* is suppressed as the density is reduced), a trend opposite to that found for the paramagnetic case over the same density interval.

conclusion.content: For the fully spin-polarized (ferromagnetic, ζ = 1) two-dimensional uniform electron liquid (2D-UEL) studied in the metallic density range 1 ≤ r_s ≤ 5, VMC and fixed-node DMC calculations performed for simulation cells containing N = 151 electrons indicate that the quasiparticle effective mass m* decreases as r_s increases (i.e., m* is suppressed as the density is reduced), a trend opposite to that found for the paramagnetic case over the same density interval.

premises.content: For the fully spin-polarized (ζ = 1) two-dimensional uniform electron liquid (2D-UEL) in the metallic density range 1 ≤ r_s ≤ 5, Pauli exclusion forces all N electrons into a single spin band, which reduces the available phase space for close electron approaches and therefore suppresses short-range electron–electron correlation relative to the paramagnetic case; this suppression of correlation leads to reduced many-body renormalization of the quasiparticle dispersion and consequently to a decreasing quasiparticle effective mass m* as r_s increases (density decreases), consistent with the authors' VMC and DMC observations performed for simulation cells with N = 151 electrons.

steps.reasoning: Start from the upstream result that finite-size extrapolation using an empirical $N^{-3/2}$ scaling permits thermodynamic-limit estimates of $m^{*}$ when combined with careful band fitting and nodal improvements (upstream conclusion: finite-size extrapolation follows $N^{-3/2}$ scaling).

Define the ferromagnetic ($\zeta=1$) 2D-UEL system studied: $\zeta$ is the spin polarization defined by $\zeta=(N_{\uparrow}-N_{\downarrow})/N$, and for $\zeta=1$ all electrons occupy a single spin band; the authors performed VMC and DMC calculations for ferromagnetic systems at densities $1\le r_{s}\le5$ using $N=151$ electrons in the simulation cell.

Describe the computed trend for the ferromagnetic case: after computing quasiparticle bands, fitting (using stable quartic fits), and applying finite-size considerations as appropriate, the authors plot $m^{*}$ versus $r_{s}$ for the ferromagnetic 2D-UEL (their Fig. 8) and observe that $m^{*}$ decreases as $r_{s}$ increases over the studied density range, i.e., $m^{*}$ is suppressed as the density is reduced in the ferromagnetic system.
Fig. 8

Explain the physical interpretation given by the authors: spin polarization (ferromagnetism) suppresses electron-electron correlation due to the Pauli exclusion principle preventing electrons of the same spin from approaching, and this suppression of correlation reduces the renormalization of effective mass; therefore, unlike the paramagnetic case where correlation enhances $m^{*}$ at low density, the ferromagnetic system shows $m^{*}$ decreasing with reduced density.

Conclude that the VMC and DMC calculations indicate a suppression of $m^{*}$ with increasing $r_{s}$ for fully spin-polarized 2D-UEL within the metallic density range, which is behavior opposite to the paramagnetic case across the same densities.

motivating_questions.content: Spin polarization alters both exchange and correlation: Pauli exclusion restricts same-spin pair correlations, modifying the balance between correlation-induced mass enhancement and screening-induced suppression. Prior theoretical treatments and experiments reported effective-mass suppression in spin-polarized two-dimensional systems, but quantitative, parameter-free confirmation for the ideal 2D-UEL over $1\le r_{s}\le5$ was lacking because of finite-size, nodal and methodological uncertainties. An ab initio QMC determination clarifies the trend and its contrast with the paramagnetic case.

paper:1129109501803233307.en_title: Quantum Monte Carlo study of the quasiparticle effective mass of the two-dimensional uniform electron liquid
paper:1129109501803233307.zh_title: 二维均匀电子气准粒子有效质量的量子蒙特卡罗研究
